entrypoints.kind_note: Interactive bash sub-process spawned via 'bash --rcfile <(cat <<RCFILE ... RCFILE) -i < /dev/tty'. Closest enum value is 'cli', but the schema also allows 'other' which more accurately captures this nested-shell pattern.
side_effects: override next/prev/show/quit/exit shell builtins to exit codes 0/97/98/99
execute any subsequent learner-typed command with the learner's normal user authority
entrypoints.termination_condition: Subshell exit. Navigation overrides return 0 (next), 97 (show), 98 (prev), 99 (quit/exit). If rc=99, practice() calls 'exit 0' directly (script-level exit, bypassing the lesson loop).
entrypoints.lifecycle_contract: synchronous foreground subprocess; one subshell per lesson
entrypoints.authority_followup: subshell-safety-claim-vs-shell-authority — sandbox boundary is `cd $SANDBOX` + HISTFILE only; learner retains normal filesystem authority inside the subshell. Phase 6 owns full analysis.

citations.evidence: next() { builtin exit 0; } prev() { builtin exit 98; } show() { builtin exit 97; } quit() { builtin exit 99; } exit() { builtin exit 99; }
Everything happens in ~/.shelltutor; you cannot break anything

loops.iteration_mechanism: C-style for-loop over a fixed lesson array (size 11)
termination_conditions: i reaches total (array exhausted; 11 lessons including welcome and finale)
rc=99 from practice() escapes the loop via 'exit 0' in practice() (shelltutor:136-140)
